questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700; text-align: justify;">Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select &#39;No Error&#39;.</span></p>

<p><span style="color: rgb(34, 34, 34);   text-align: justify;">Justin Bieber&rsquo;s performance was given (A)/ a standing ovation by the (B)/ people who has come to hear him.(C)/ No errorD)</span></p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined word in a sentence.</p>

<p>Despite her&nbsp;<u>earnest</u>&nbsp;efforts, she could not find a job.</p>
<strong>Select the word that is spelled correctly.</strong>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence.</span></p>

<p style="">How long is it since you <u>have painted</u> a landscape?</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.</p>

<p>The entrepreneur decided to <u>throw caution to the wind</u>.&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select &lsquo;No substitution&rsquo;.&nbsp;</strong></p>

<p>Are you trying to say that you are stupid <u>so</u> proud of it?</p>
Identify the idiom with a false meaning:
options: Take it with a grain of salt - To fully believe something. /
&nbsp;On thin ice - In a risky or dangerous situation. /
Hit the nail on the head - To be exactly right. /
Jump the gun - To act too soon or prematurely. /

questions.statement:
<p>Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.</p>

<p>______&nbsp;attending the class, he was listening to the cricket commentary.</p>

<p>Directions: Each item in this section consists of a sentence with an underlined word followed by four words/groups of words. Select the option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.</p>

<p>The manager&#39;s decision was <strong>prudent</strong> considering the circumstances.</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Select the option that is opposite in meaning to the given bold word and mark your response accordingly.</strong></p>

<p>He was in the right place in a <u><strong>prominent</strong></u> position but he has improved in him.</p>
